idea-plugin.name: php-periscope
idea-plugin.description:
Live observability + time-travel debugger for Laravel. Pauses any request, shows every variable, SQL query, log line, dispatched job, fired event, cache hit, Redis command, and outbound HTTP call that occurred up to the paused line — and lets you scrub backward in time.

Connects to periscope-daemon over DAP-stdio. Supports breakpoints, step over / into / out, step back, variables, watches, evaluate.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.7

Six bugs found in a v0.1.6 audit, plus two features previously deferred to v0.2.

  • Daemon-spawn errors now surface to the user — pre-flight checks that periscope-daemon exists (absolute path or on PATH). Previously a missing or non-executable daemon caused the Debug window to open then close with no visible error (same silent-failure class as the v0.1.6 getState bug).
  • Breakpoints set before the session starts are no longer dropped on first run — the handler buffers registrations and flushes them after the DAP initialized event. Previously every pre-session breakpoint silently failed and had to be toggled off-on after the toolwindow opened.
  • URL-encoded file paths now work — breakpoints in project paths with spaces (My%20Project/...) no longer silently miss. The handler decodes via VirtualFileManager.extractPath with a manual URLDecoder fallback.
  • Listen-mode WatchService no longer leaks on session cancel — the per-attempt coroutine scope is parented to a project Disposable, so Stop / project-close tears down the inotify handle cleanly.
  • DAP reader hardened — case-insensitive Content-Length lookup per spec, safe cast on incoming messages (a non-JSON-object line on stdout no longer crashes the reader), and the reader catches Throwable rather than just IOException so shutdownPending() always runs.
  • Step Back button no longer flickers grey on session open — toolbar enable now uses a sticky sessionInitialized flag instead of dap.isAlive directly.

New features:

  • ConsoleView wiring — PHP code output (echo, var_dump, etc.) reconstructed from the trace now appears in the Debug → Console panel with full ANSI / search / fold support. stderr-class output is rendered in red. Buffered before the platform requests the console so startup output isn't lost.
  • Run-to-Position (Ctrl+Alt+F9) — properly implemented via implicit continue-until-line on top of the existing trace replay. Previously fell back to Resume.

Settings editor migrated to the non-deprecated addBrowseFolderListener(TextBrowseFolderListener(...)) API for forward compatibility past 2026.1.

0.1.6

  • Fix: clicking Debug with the Periscope config selected did nothing — PeriscopeRunConfiguration.getState() returned null, which makes the IntelliJ platform silently abort the launch before PeriscopeDebuggerRunner.execute() is ever called. Now returns a no-op RunProfileState so the platform proceeds to invoke the runner.

0.1.5

  • Fix duplicate-scheme rejection — when a Periscope run config already existed in .idea/runConfigurations/Periscope.xml, the platform silently rejected the auto-seeded one and the resulting "Switch to Periscope" balloon action did nothing. Now: skip seeding when an on-disk or in-memory Periscope config is detected, and resolve the live settings at click-time so the dropdown actually switches.
  • Always show the "Switch to Periscope" balloon on every project open when the dropdown isn't already on Periscope. Includes a "Don't show again" per-project opt-out.

0.1.4

  • Auto-listen on Debug — pressing the Debug button with no trace yet now watches the trace directory and attaches the moment the next .cptrace lands. No tool window, no toggle. (Fixes #1)
  • Auto-pick the latest trace — leave Trace file blank to replay the newest trace in /tmp/periscope. Explicit paths still work for historical replay; missing paths surface a clear error within ~1s instead of hanging. (Fixes #2)
  • Pre-filled config name — new Periscope run configs default to "Periscope" instead of an empty Name field. (Fixes #3)
  • Zero-config setup — on first project open we silently seed a Periscope run config in .idea/runConfigurations/ and nudge the user with a one-time balloon if they're currently on Xdebug/another config. Idempotent — does nothing on subsequent opens.
  • Settings → Tools → Periscope — application-level toggle to disable auto-seeding for power users.
  • Suppress green Play button for Periscope configs — only the Debug bug action is meaningful for trace replay.

0.1.0

  • Initial scaffold — registers the periscope debug type and Run/Debug configuration.

action.description: Move the trace cursor one step backward (periscope time-travel)
Continue replay in reverse until the previous breakpoint (periscope time-travel)
